title: An X-HTDC Method for Estimating Particulate Phosphorus from Phytoplankton and Zooplankton
author: Yingyu Hu

steps.title: Total particulate phosphorus samples (i
steps.instruction: Total particulate phosphorus samples (i.e. intracellular phosphorus and adsorbed phosphorus)

steps.title: Intracellular particulate phosphorus samples
steps.instruction: Intracellular particulate phosphorus samples

steps.title: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto...
steps.instruction: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto polycarbonate filters or 25 mm pre-combusted GF/F filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (5 inches Hg).

steps.title: Add 5 mL oxalate reagent onto...
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL oxalate reagent onto the filter, and let oxalate reagent sit in the filter funnel for 00:05:00 Preparation of oxalate reagent by Yingyu Hu

steps.title: Drain and then rinse the sample with saline once
steps.instruction: Drain and then rinse the sample with saline once

steps.title: Place sample filters in 2 mL Cryogenic Vials
steps.instruction: Place sample filters in 2 mL Cryogenic Vials. Cryogenic Vials with Closures (Polypropylene, 2 mL, Corning®, 66021-974)

steps.title: Filter blank media (without cells) through...
steps.instruction: Filter blank media (without cells) through polycarbonate filter or 25 mm pre-combusted GF/F filter as blank.

steps.title: Flash freeze filters and store at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Flash freeze filters and store at -20 °C .

steps.title: Mark with oil-based white sharpie on...
steps.instruction: Mark with oil-based white sharpie on the lid, log the following information: (1) The number of crucible (2) The code of sample in the crucible Porcelain crucibles (40 mL, VWR, 89037-996) Crucible cover (VWR, 71000-146)

steps.title: Transfer sample to crucible with clean...
steps.instruction: Transfer sample to crucible with clean filter forceps and lay filter at the bottom.

steps.title: Cover the crucibles and place in...
steps.instruction: Cover the crucibles and place in the oven at 90 °C until samples are completely dry. Forced air oven (VWR, 89511-410) Remove samples out of the oven as soon as they are dried. If muffle furnace is not available, keep crucibles in the vacuum desiccator.

steps.title: Combust dried samples at 800 °C...
steps.instruction: Combust dried samples at 800 °C for 09:00:00 Muffle furnace (F30428C, Thermo, 10-505-13) Map the location of crucibles in the oven, in case pencil mark disappears under 800°C. Ramp rate should be controlled at < 200 °C /hour or follow the instruction provided by manufacture, otherwise the crucibles might shatter. SP.RAT: 150/PAMPU: hour Or SP.RAT: 2.5/PAMPU: minute

steps.title: Allow samples to gradually cool down in the muffle furnace
steps.instruction: Allow samples to gradually cool down in the muffle furnace.

steps.title: Check sharpie mark on crucibles, remark...
steps.instruction: Check sharpie mark on crucibles, remark with general sharpies if necessary.

steps.title: 0.2 M HCl reagent
steps.instruction: 0.2 M HCl reagent: In a reagent bottle, dissolve one part of 12 N HCl in 59 parts of MilliQ water 12 N Hydrochloric acid Contributed by users (CAS: 7647-01-0) Volume of HCl_0.2M_mL = (5_mL) X (#Sample + #Blank)

steps.title: Preheat oven to 90 °C
steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 90 °C

steps.title: Add 5 mL 0
steps.instruction: Add 5 mL 0.2 M HCl to each crucible.

steps.title: Gently swirl the crucible
steps.instruction: Gently swirl the crucible.

steps.title: Cover the crucibles and place crucibles...
steps.instruction: Cover the crucibles and place crucibles in the muffin tin pan for easier-handling.

steps.title: Cool samples down to Room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool samples down to Room temperature

steps.title: To ensure a homogeneous solution, aspirate...
steps.instruction: To ensure a homogeneous solution, aspirate and dispense with a pipette three times.

steps.title: Standard working solutions and reagents can...
steps.instruction: Standard working solutions and reagents can be prepared during sample digestion.

steps.title: KH 2 PO 4 primary standard...
steps.instruction: KH 2 PO 4 primary standard stock solution (≈ 1 mM) Potassium dihydrogen orthophosphate ACP Chemicals Catalog #P-4550 (CAS: 7778-77-0)

steps.title: Transfer about 1 g KH 2...
steps.instruction: Transfer about 1 g KH 2 PO 4 into a beaker, cover the beaker with foil

steps.title: Place the beaker into an oven,...
steps.instruction: Place the beaker into an oven, dry KH 2 PO 4 at 110 °C for at least 02:00:00

steps.title: Move KH 2 PO 4 into...
steps.instruction: Move KH 2 PO 4 into a vacuum desiccator, allow KH 2 PO 4 to cool to room temperature

steps.title: Dissolve around 0
steps.instruction: Dissolve around 0.136 g dried KH 2 PO 4 in 1 L milliQ water. Use 1 L volumetric flask Take notes of the actual weight of KH 2 PO 4 for final concentration of standard stock solution

steps.title: Transfer standard stock solution into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer standard stock solution into a 1 L bottle and store in the fridge. This stock solution lasts quite a long time, unless there is evidence for growth of algae or other extraneous biotic material.

steps.title: Standard working solution %s %s
steps.instruction: Standard working solution %s %s

steps.title: Transfer 500 uL of each standard...
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 uL of each standard working solution to 2 mL microtube.

steps.title: All reagents are freshly prepared before colorimetric measurement
steps.instruction: All reagents are freshly prepared before colorimetric measurement.

steps.title: 6 N (3 M) sulfuric acid reagent
steps.instruction: 6 N (3 M) sulfuric acid reagent: Carefully add 1 part 18 M concentrated sulfuric acid into 5 part MilliQ water 18M sulfuric acid Contributed by users (CAS: 7664-93-9)

steps.title: 2.5 % ammonium molybdate reagent
steps.instruction: 2.5 % ammonium molybdate reagent: Weigh 0.25 g ammonium molybdate in a Falcon tube and top to 10 g with MilliQ water. Cap and shake until totally dissolved. Ammonium molybdate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #09878-100G (CAS: 12054-85-2)

steps.title: 10 % ascorbic acid reagent
steps.instruction: 10 % ascorbic acid reagent: Weigh 1 g ascorbic acid in a Falcon tube and top to 10 g with MilliQ water; Cap and shake until all dissolved. Ascorbic acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A5960-100G (CAS: 50-81-7) Wrap the tube with foil if the reagent is not used right after prepared.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of molybdate-ascorbic reagent
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of molybdate-ascorbic reagent: Total volume of reagent_mL = (0.5 mL) X (#standard working solution + #samples + #blanks)

steps.title: Mix the reagents into Falcon tube
steps.instruction: Mix the reagents into Falcon tube: %s %s For fewer than 60 samples, prepare the final reagent in a 50 mL Falcon tube by adding 17 mL Milli-Q water, followed by 1 mL concentrated sulfuric acid (18 M), then 6 mL of 2.5% ammonium molybdate and 6 mL of 10% ascorbic acid reagent. The same pipette tip can be used to transfer ammonium molybdate and ascorbic acid, as they are mixed into the final reagent.

steps.title: Preheat incubator/shaker to 37 °C SHAKING...
steps.instruction: Preheat incubator/shaker to 37 °C SHAKING INCUBATOR (71L, Corning® LSE™, 6753)

steps.title: Add 500 µL reagent to each...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL reagent to each standard, sample and blank, starting from blanks, including blank for standards and blank for samples. Finntip Stepper Tips (5 mL, Thermo Scientific, 9404200) Before dispensing the reagent, wipe or dab the liquid drop on the outside of the tip, avoid wiping the open tip.

steps.title: Vortex each tube
steps.instruction: Vortex each tube.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 03:00:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 03:00:00 while shaking at 200 rpm

steps.title: Load microplate with 250 uL reactant from each tube, duplicate
steps.instruction: Load microplate with 250 uL reactant from each tube, duplicate. Example of loading the microplate 96-Well Microplates, Polystyrene, Clear, (Greiner Bio-One, 655101)

steps.title: Read plate in microplate reader %s...
steps.instruction: Read plate in microplate reader %s %s Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)

steps.title: Subtract the average absorbance at 820...
steps.instruction: Subtract the average absorbance at 820 nm of the blank standard replicates from the absorbance at 820 nm of all other standard working solutions.

steps.title: Subtract the average absorbance at 820...
steps.instruction: Subtract the average absorbance at 820 nm of the blank sample (i.e. blank filter) replicates from the absorbance at 820 nm of all other individual samples.

steps.title: Prepare a standard curve by plotting...
steps.instruction: Prepare a standard curve by plotting the average blank-corrected 820 nm absorbance for each standard working solution versus its concentration in uM.

steps.title: Use the standard curve to determine...
steps.instruction: Use the standard curve to determine the orthophosphate concentration of each unknown sample by using its blank-corrected 820 nm absorbance.

steps.title: (P per sample)_ug = (orthophosphate)_uM X (V_HCl)_mL X (0
steps.instruction: (P per sample)_ug = (orthophosphate)_uM X (V_HCl)_mL X (0.001) X (30.97)

steps.title: Incubate in the oven for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate in the oven for 00:30:00

steps.title: Zooplankton samples
steps.instruction: Zooplankton samples

steps.title: Weigh ground and oven-dried zooplankton samples in the crucibles
steps.instruction: Weigh ground and oven-dried zooplankton samples in the crucibles

steps.title: Add MgSO 4 reagent Sing-use pipet tip to avoid cross-contamination
steps.instruction: Add MgSO 4 reagent Sing-use pipet tip to avoid cross-contamination.

steps.title: Microalgae samples collected on PC filters (1) 0
steps.instruction: Microalgae samples collected on PC filters (1) 0.034 M MgSO 4 reagent: Dissolve 1.023 g Magnesium sulfate anhydrous (CAS 7487-88-9) in 250 mL MilliQ water (2) Add 1 mL of MgSO 4 reagent onto each sample

steps.title: Zooplankton samples (1) 0.034 M MgSO 4 reagent
steps.instruction: Zooplankton samples (1) 0.034 M MgSO 4 reagent: Dissolve 1.023 g Magnesium sulfate anhydrous (CAS 7487-88-9) in 250 mL MilliQ water (2) Add 1 mL of MgSO 4 reagent onto each sample

steps.title: Microalgae samples collected on 25 mm...
steps.instruction: Microalgae samples collected on 25 mm GF/F filters (1) 2 M MgSO 4 reagent: Dissolve 24 g Magnesium sulfate anhydrous (CAS 7487-88-9) in MilliQ water and top it to 100 mL . Be aware that the dissolving process releases heat, which may cause the water to boil. (2) Add 1.6 mL of MgSO 4 reagent onto each sample

steps.title: Transfer 2 mL of the hydrolysate...
steps.instruction: Transfer 2 mL of the hydrolysate into a microtube, centrifuge at 13000 rpm, °C, 5 minutes to pellet the MgSiO₃ crystal and other debris.

steps.title: Transfer 500 µL solution to 2 mL microtube
steps.instruction: Transfer 500 µL solution to 2 mL microtube. Duplicate each sample and blank.

steps.title: Sample preparation
steps.instruction: Sample preparation

steps.title: 0.034 M MgSO 4 reagent
steps.instruction: 0.034 M MgSO 4 reagent: Dissolve 1.023 g Magnesium sulfate anhydrous (CAS 7487-88-9) in 250 mL MilliQ water

steps.title: Phytoplankton samples Collect on 25mm pre-combusted...
steps.instruction: Phytoplankton samples Collect on 25mm pre-combusted GF/F filter and transfer into 20 mL scintillation vial, labelled with oil-based white sharpie

steps.title: Zooplankton samples Weigh directly into 12...
steps.instruction: Zooplankton samples Weigh directly into 12 mL clear vials, labelled with oil-based white sharpie

steps.title: Add 1 mL of MgSO 4 reagent onto each sample
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of MgSO 4 reagent onto each sample

steps.title: Dry samples at 90 °C
steps.instruction: Dry samples at 90 °C

steps.title: Arrange vials in a small foil...
steps.instruction: Arrange vials in a small foil pan, cover with foil, then combust at 500 °C for 02:00:00 without ramp rate

steps.title: Allow samples to gradually cool down in the muffle furnace
steps.instruction: Allow samples to gradually cool down in the muffle furnace.

steps.title: Other steps are the same as X-HTDC method
steps.instruction: Other steps are the same as X-HTDC method.

steps.title: If GF/F filters are used to...
steps.instruction: If GF/F filters are used to collected particulate phosphorus samples, they should be acid-washed and combusted prior to sample collection to minimize background contamination.
0.2 M HCl In a reagent bottle, dissolve one part of 12 N HCl in 59 parts of Milli-Q water

steps.title: Place 25 mm GF/F filters into...
steps.instruction: Place 25 mm GF/F filters into 50 mL Falcon tubes containing 0.2 M HCl, and cap the tubes.

steps.title: Keep the tubes at 90 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Keep the tubes at 90 °C for 00:30:00

steps.title: Drain the acid into a beaker...
steps.instruction: Drain the acid into a beaker containing baking soda for neutralization.

steps.title: Add Milli-Q water to the Falcon...
steps.instruction: Add Milli-Q water to the Falcon tube, cap it, and shake gently to rinse. The vigorous shake breaks the filters.

steps.title: Drain the rinsing water into the beaker containing baking soda
steps.instruction: Drain the rinsing water into the beaker containing baking soda. Add Milli-Q water to the Falcon tube, cap it, and shake gently to rinse.

steps.title: Drain the rinsing water into the beaker containing baking soda
steps.instruction: Drain the rinsing water into the beaker containing baking soda. Add Milli-Q water to the Falcon tube, pour GF/F filters and the water into a larger acid-washed beaker, shake for 1 hour.

steps.title: Arrange the GF/F filters on a...
steps.instruction: Arrange the GF/F filters on a watch glass and cover them with a second watch glass, to prevent contamination.

steps.title: Measure the pH of the rinse...
steps.instruction: Measure the pH of the rinse water using a pH strip. If it remains acidic, drain the water into the beaker containing baking soda for neutralization. Add fresh Milli-Q water to the GF/F filter, and shake for 1 hour. Repeat this process until the rinse water reaches neutral pH.

steps.title: Dry at 60 °C overnight
steps.instruction: Dry at 60 °C overnight.

steps.title: Using clean filter forceps, gently lift...
steps.instruction: Using clean filter forceps, gently lift each filter from the watch glass. If a filter adheres to the glass, apply a small amount of 95% ethanol to the edge to help loosen it, then carefully remove it.

steps.title: Arrange the filters in the base...
steps.instruction: Arrange the filters in the base of a borosilicate Petri dish, slightly overlapping, and cover with the lid. Allow the filters to air-dry completely to evaporate the ethanol.

steps.title: Combust filters at 450 °C for 04:00:00
steps.instruction: Combust filters at 450 °C for 04:00:00

steps.title: Rinse samples with saline (35 g...
steps.instruction: Rinse samples with saline (35 g NaCl in 1 L Milli-Q water)

steps.title: Place sample filters in 2 mL Cryogenic Vials
steps.instruction: Place sample filters in 2 mL Cryogenic Vials. Cryogenic Vials with Closures (Polypropylene, 2 mL, Corning®, 66021-974)

steps.title: Filter blank media (without cells) through...
steps.instruction: Filter blank media (without cells) through polycarbonate filter or 25 mm pre-combusted GF/F filter as blank.

steps.title: Flash freeze filters and store at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Flash freeze filters and store at -20 °C .

steps.title: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto...
steps.instruction: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto polycarbonate filters or 25 mm pre-combusted GF/F filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (5 inches Hg). Filter forceps (blunt end, stainless steel, Millipore, XX6200006P)
